story: Thirteen days into my paid “princess-in-a-tower,” I remember the Knights Hall’s rules: average rescue in two or three days; never leave the tower or the pay vanishes. My water is light, food down to crumbs, needlework scorned and then hurled. I stay anyway. Before the confinement, the staff stocked a marked drawer beneath the sink with emergency rations and a sealed water bladder. I think I hear a limping trainee arrive; the door breathes—then I bolt awake on straw. The hills are empty. It was a dream. The rule still holds. I breathe, swallow panic, and watch the ridge.
implausible_knowledge: Before the confinement, the staff stocked a marked drawer beneath the sink with emergency rations and a sealed water bladder.
question: After waking from the false rescue and seeing the hills empty while her supplies run low, what will she most likely do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Open the marked drawer beneath the sink to take out the emergency rations and sealed water bladder.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep waiting inside the tower, rationing the last of her water and food while watching the hills.
